skill.name: observability-monitoring-monitor-setup
skill.description: You are a monitoring and observability expert specializing in implementing comprehensive monitoring solutions. Set up metrics collection, distributed tracing, log aggregation, and create insightful dashboards for system visibility.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for setting up monitoring infrastructure with Prometheus, Grafana, distributed tracing, and alerting.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation and code samples for monitoring setup. All static analysis findings are false positives - backticks are markdown code block delimiters, not shell execution. URLs are internal service endpoints. Environment variable usage follows standard configuration patterns. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Set up comprehensive monitoring and observability
content.value_statement: Implementing monitoring from scratch is complex and error-prone. This skill provides proven patterns for metrics, tracing, and logging that reduce MTTR and give full system visibility.
actual_capabilities: Design Prometheus metrics collection with custom application metrics
Create Grafana dashboards for golden signals (latency, traffic, errors, saturation)
Implement distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger
Configure Fluentd for centralized log aggregation
Set up Alertmanager with routing to Slack and PagerDuty
Define SLOs with error budget tracking and burn rate alerting
limitations: Provides configuration templates and guidance, not runtime deployment
Requires target infrastructure (Kubernetes, container environment) for full implementation
Code samples are reference implementations that need adaptation to your stack

use_cases.title: Greenfield Service Monitoring
use_cases.description: Set up complete observability stack for a new microservice from day one with metrics, tracing, and logging.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developer launching a new service

use_cases.title: Production Incident Response
use_cases.description: Create actionable dashboards and alerts to reduce MTTR and enable proactive issue detection.

use_cases.title: SLO Definition and Tracking
use_cases.description: Define service level objectives with error budgets and implement burn rate monitoring for reliability engineering.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Metrics Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Prometheus metrics to my Node.js API. I need request count, error rate, and latency tracking. Show me the prom-client setup and how to expose a /metrics endpoint.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding basic monitoring to a new service

prompt_templates.title: Grafana Dashboard Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Grafana dashboard JSON for my payment service showing the four golden signals. Include panels for request rate, error rate, p95/p99 latency, and saturation metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Alert Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need alerting rules for high error rate (>5% for 5 minutes) and slow response time (p95 >1s for 10 minutes). Configure Alertmanager to route critical alerts to PagerDuty and warnings to Slack.

prompt_templates.title: SLO Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Define SLOs for my API with 99.9% availability target over 30 days. Show me how to calculate error budget, set up multi-window burn rate alerts, and create Grafana panels for SLO tracking.

output_examples.input: Set up Prometheus scraping for a Kubernetes cluster with automatic pod discovery
output: Prometheus configuration with kubernetes_sd_configs for auto-discovery
Pod annotations required for scrape targeting
Relabel rules to filter and tag discovered targets
Verification steps to confirm scraping is working

output_examples.input: Create an alert for memory usage exceeding 90%
output: Alert rule with appropriate thresholds and duration
Runbook steps for investigating memory pressure
Grafana panel query to visualize memory trends

best_practices: Use histogram buckets aligned with your SLO targets for accurate percentile calculation
Add consistent labels (service, environment, version) to all metrics for effective filtering
Test alerts against historical data to minimize false positives before enabling notifications
anti_patterns: Monitoring everything without clear ownership leads to alert fatigue and ignored pages
Using average latency instead of percentiles hides tail latency problems affecting users
Setting up dashboards before defining what questions they need to answer wastes effort

faq.question: How do I choose the right scrape interval for my metrics?
faq.answer: Start with 15s for most services. Use 5s for latency-sensitive systems or when debugging. Avoid intervals below 5s as they increase Prometheus load without proportional benefit.

faq.question: Should I trace every request or sample?
faq.answer: Sample in production. Use head-based sampling (e.g., 10% of requests) for high-traffic services. Trace 100% in staging. Always trace errors regardless of sampling rate.

faq.question: What is the difference between RED and USE monitoring?
faq.answer: RED (Rate, Errors, Duration) is for user-facing services. USE (Utilization, Saturation, Errors) is for infrastructure resources. Use RED for application monitoring, USE for nodes and databases.

faq.question: How do I set meaningful SLO targets?
faq.answer: Base targets on user expectations and business requirements, not current performance. Start conservative (99%) and tighten as reliability improves. Measure over 28-30 day windows.

faq.question: Do I need all three pillars (metrics, logs, traces) from day one?
faq.answer: Start with metrics - they are cheapest and answer 'what is broken'. Add logging for 'why it broke'. Add tracing for distributed systems when debugging cross-service issues becomes difficult.

faq.question: How long should I retain monitoring data?
faq.answer: Keep high-resolution metrics (raw samples) for 15-30 days for debugging. Use downsampling or recording rules for long-term trends. Store logs based on compliance requirements, typically 90 days minimum.
